config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because once individual rights are treated as secondary, abuse becomes too easy to justify. From my perspective, a healthy society is built by protecting people’s dignity, freedom, and legal rights first, then balancing those rights with the common good. Society matters a lot, but it should not be used as an excuse to silence or sacrifice individuals unfairly.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika Serikat, itu terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk penyalahgunaan kuasa. Hak individu seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan dari tindakan sewenang-wenang justru menjadi batas yang mencegah masyarakat atau negara mengorbankan orang tertentu demi alasan yang terdengar “demi kebaikan bersama.” Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, bukan sebaliknya. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat bisa berubah menjadi tekanan mayoritas yang menindas minoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not take priority over individual rights as a blanket rule, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “the public interest” can be used to pressure people into silence or unfair treatment. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every situation, because public health, safety, and order sometimes require real limits. The key point is that those limits must be narrow, transparent, and accountable, not a free pass for authorities or majorities to override people whenever it is convenient. A strong society is one that protects individuals first and only restricts them when there is a clear and justified reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai aturan umum, karena menurut pengalaman nilai-nilai Amerika, hak individu justru harus menjadi batas utama terhadap kekuasaan mayoritas dan negara. Argumen tentang kesehatan publik, keselamatan, dan ketertiban memang sah, tetapi saya tidak setuju jika itu dipakai untuk menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu secara otomatis, karena di situlah penyalahgunaan paling mudah masuk. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan orang demi “kepentingan umum,” melainkan yang mampu membuktikan bahwa pembatasan hak itu benar-benar perlu, sempit, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah berubah menjadi alasan yang terdengar mulia tetapi menindas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over individual rights, because in Indonesia that kind of logic can easily be used to justify pressure, silence, and unfair treatment. I do recognize that public health, safety, and order sometimes require limits, but those limits must be narrow and accountable, not a blank check for the state or the majority. What I reject is the idea that “the common good” should come first by default, since that often ends up protecting power more than people. A fair society is one that can defend the public interest without treating individual rights as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika Serikat, hak individu harus menjadi pagar utama terhadap kekuasaan mayoritas dan negara. Saya paham argumen tentang kepentingan publik, kesehatan, dan ketertiban, tetapi justru di situlah bahaya muncul: begitu “demi masyarakat” dijadikan alasan default, penyalahgunaan jadi sangat mudah dibenarkan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan orang demi kebaikan bersama, melainkan yang bisa membuktikan bahwa pembatasan hak itu benar-benar perlu, sempit, dan dapat dipertanggungjawabkan. Jadi, kepentingan sosial penting, tetapi tidak layak diprioritaskan di atas hak dasar individu secara otomatis.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
